column1: Which primary germ layer gives rise to the epidermis of the skin?
The connective tissue of the dermis is primarily derived from which germ layer?
What is the name of the temporary, outermost layer of simple squamous epithelium formed by the surface ectoderm in the fourth week?
During fetal development, the layer of stem cells located beneath the periderm that replenishes the epidermis is called the _____ layer.
In which week of development does the basal layer produce an intermediate layer between itself and the periderm?
By which week of development is the intermediate layer replaced by the three definitive layers of the outer epidermis?
What clinical term describes a newborn infant born with a persistent, shell-like periderm that failed to shed properly?
From which embryonic structure do melanocytes (pigment cells) originate before migrating into the epidermis?
During which weeks of embryonic development do melanocytes first appear in the epidermis?
What is the embryonic origin of Langerhans cells found in the epidermis?
Which signaling pathway is essential for the initial establishment of the ectodermal hair placode?
The dermis of the trunk is derived from the somatic layer of the lateral plate mesoderm and the _____ subdivision of the somites.
Unlike the trunk, the dermis of the face and neck is derived from which embryonic source?
What structure is formed when the superficial layer of the dermis projects into the overlying epidermis during the third month?
What are the downward projections of the epidermis that interdigitate with the dermal papillae called?
Hair follicles first originate as rod-like downgrowths of the stratum germinativum called _____ _____.
Which structure, consisting of a hillock of dermis, indents the base of the hair follicle to provide nourishment and signaling?
Sebaceous glands typically develop as diverticula from which structure?
What waterproof, protective coating covers the fetus, composed of sebum, desquamated epidermal cells, and periderm remnants?
Which type of sweat gland develops as an independent downgrowth from the fetal epidermis, unrelated to hair follicles?
At approximately which week of development do eccrine sweat glands first appear on the palms and soles?
Which glands in the axillae and pubic region develop in association with hair follicles and produce odorous secretions at puberty?
The specialized cells in sweat glands that contract to expel secretions are called _____ cells.
From which specialized layer of the nail fold does the nail plate grow?
The skin accounts for approximately what percentage of total body mass?
What is the histological classification of the epithelium that forms the epidermis?
Which layer of the epidermis is characterized by mitotically active stem cells?
The 'prickle cell layer' of the epidermis is histologically known as the _____ _____.
Which epidermal layer is characterized by cells containing intensely staining keratohyalin granules?
In which specific type of skin is the stratum lucidum typically visible?
The stratum corneum is composed of flattened, dead cells filled with the protein _____.
Which layer of the dermis consists of loose connective tissue and contains a high density of capillaries and nerve endings?
The deeper, thicker layer of the dermis characterized by coarse, irregular bundles of collagen fibers is the _____ layer.
What is the histological term for the layer of adipose tissue located deep to the dermis?
Which smooth muscle, associated with hair follicles, causes 'goosebumps' upon contraction?
By what mechanism do sebaceous glands release their oily product?
The process by which the outermost cells of the epidermis are shed is called _____.
Which signaling molecule's antagonism by Noggin is required for the onset of myogenic and dermal commitment in the somite?
Mutation in the $EDA/EDAR$ signaling pathway results in which clinical condition characterized by the absence of hair and sweat glands?
What is the function of the eccrine sweat glands?
Which cell type in the epidermis functions as part of the immune system by presenting antigens to T lymphocytes?
In the context of hair development, the expanded base of the hair peg is called the _____ _____.
Which gene family, specifically $Msx1$ and $Msx2$, is required for the development of skin appendages beyond the bud stage?
What clinical condition, a benign tumor of hair follicle matrix cells, is caused by constitutive activation of the $Wnt/\beta-catenin$ pathway?
The inner layer of cells in a developing sweat gland differentiates into secretory cells, while the outer layer becomes _____ cells.
During which trimester does the dermis differentiate into its definitive papillary and reticular layers?
What determines the specific shape and pattern of an ectodermal derivative (like a hair or gland) during development?
At approximately how many weeks do the fingernails reach the tips of the digits?
At approximately how many weeks do the toenails reach the tips of the digits?
What is the name of the initial thickened areas of ectoderm that mark the beginning of skin appendage development?
Which embryonic layer covers the entire surface of the three-dimensional embryo after body folding is complete, excluding the umbilical region?
The mammary glands are considered a specialized type of which skin derivative?
Which cell layer is the source of 'amniocytes' collected during amniocentesis?
The unspecialized epithelial cells surrounding the dermal papilla in a hair follicle are known as the _____.
Which sweat glands only become functional and begin to secrete in response to sex steroids at puberty?
What term refers to the process where the bilaminar embryonic disc is transformed into a trilaminar embryo?
In thick skin, the duct of an eccrine sweat gland takes what shape as it traverses the stratum corneum?
The embryoblast differentiates into which two layers to form the bilaminar embryonic disc?
From which specific embryonic layer are all three primary germ layers ($Ectoderm$, $Mesoderm$, $Endoderm$) derived?
What structure is the site of cell ingression during gastrulation?
Which layer of the hair follicle is directly continuous with the epidermis?
What term describes the mesenchymal-epithelial interaction that governs the formation of the integumentary system?
Melanin is produced by melanocytes and then transferred to which other cell type in the epidermis?
The dermamyotome lip that responds to $Wnt$ signals from the dorsal neural tube is called the _____ lip.
Which type of embryonic development involves the direct formation of organ rudiments from the tail bud without distinct germ layers?
